title: AquaFunded Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: AquaFunded Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). AquaFunded (3.6★, $19/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using AquaFunded — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is AquaFunded?

AquaFunded is a Dubai-based proprietary trading firm founded in 2023 that offers funded trading accounts across forex, indices, commodities, and crypto markets. The firm specializes in challenge-based funding, meaning you complete a trading evaluation phase before gaining access to real capital and profit splits. AquaFunded currently holds a 3.6/5 rating and starts at $19/month for its Instant Funding tier, making it one of the more affordable entry points in the prop trading space. Traders choose AquaFunded specifically for its multiple challenge formats (1-step, 2-step, 3-step, and instant funding), scaling pathway to $2 million funded accounts, and up to 100% profit split add-ons—though the firm's mixed Trustpilot reputation (3.2/5) means you should approach it with careful due diligence.

How to Create Your AquaFunded Account

Creating an AquaFunded account takes approximately 15-20 minutes from start to funding eligibility verification. Follow these steps:

  • Visit the website and click "Sign Up" — You'll land on AquaFunded's registration page. Choose your challenge type upfront (Instant Funding, 1-Step, 2-Step, or 3-Step) since this determines your entry price and evaluation rules.
  • Enter basic personal information — Full name, email address, and create a password. AquaFunded will send you a verification email; click the link to confirm.
  • Complete identity verification (KYC) — Provide your country of residence, date of birth, and phone number. You'll need to upload a government-issued ID (passport, national ID card, or driver's license) and sometimes a proof of address (utility bill or bank statement dated within 90 days). The KYC process usually clears within 24-48 hours.
  • Accept terms and trading rules — Review AquaFunded's trading agreement, challenge rules, and risk acknowledgments. Pay special attention to the hard-stop rules: daily loss limits, maximum loss limits per challenge phase, and the "no trading 3 minutes before market close" restrictions on forex.
  • Select your challenge parameters — Choose your target account size ($2.5K, $10K, $25K, $100K, or higher), leverage cap, and any add-ons (100% profit split typically costs extra). Confirm these can't be changed mid-challenge.
  • Complete payment — AquaFunded accepts credit/debit cards, cryptocurrency, and bank transfers. Your challenge fee is non-refundable if you fail the evaluation, but there's no monthly subscription—you only pay once per challenge attempt.

After payment, you'll receive login credentials and immediate access to your trading dashboard. Paper trading (simulated) begins right away; you're not trading with real capital until you pass the evaluation and the account transitions to funded status.

Setting Up AquaFunded for the First Time

Once logged in, AquaFunded's dashboard presents your challenge progress, account balance, open positions, and trading rules side-by-side. Here's what to configure before placing your first trade:

1. Connect your broker or trading platform — AquaFunded doesn't provide its own trading infrastructure; instead, you trade on your own MetaTrader 4, MetaTrader 5, cTrader, or mobile trading app. You'll need to link your preferred platform to your AquaFunded account through the dashboard settings. This involves generating API credentials from your broker and pasting them into AquaFunded's integration panel.

2. Review your challenge rules and risk parameters — The dashboard displays your daily loss limit, maximum loss per phase, and leverage cap in a prominent widget. Write these down. For example, if you bought a 1-Step Challenge $25K account, you might face a 5% daily loss limit ($1,250) and a 10% maximum loss to pass. These vary by challenge type.

3. Set up alerts and notifications — In Settings, enable email and/or SMS alerts for position entries, loss milestones, and account warnings. AquaFunded will notify you if you're approaching your daily loss limit—crucial for preventing accidental hard stops.

4. Customize dashboard widgets — Drag and drop widgets to match your trading style. Forex traders should prioritize the "Account Equity" and "Daily P&L" widgets. Multi-asset traders will want quick access to the "Open Positions by Instrument" view.

5. Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) — Under Security settings, turn on 2FA using an authenticator app like Google Authenticator or Authy. This protects your trading account from unauthorized access, especially critical if you're managing a funded account worth $2.5K to $2 million.

Essential Features You Should Know

Paper Trading — AquaFunded's evaluation phase runs entirely on simulated capital. Your P&L is tracked against the challenge benchmarks (e.g., reach 10% profit to pass a 2-Step Challenge), but no real money moves until you pass. This lets you test your strategy risk-free and identify whether AquaFunded's rules suit your trading.

Mobile App — AquaFunded offers iOS and Android apps that mirror the desktop dashboard. You can monitor positions, check daily P&L, and receive push notifications from anywhere. Traders working desk jobs or traveling often rely on the mobile app for quick account checks without logging into a browser.

Automated Trading Support — If you use expert advisors (EAs) or algorithmic strategies on MetaTrader, AquaFunded permits them under certain conditions (usually with advance approval). The platform supports automated order submission, position sizing, and risk management rules for algo traders—but you must disclose your system to AquaFunded and ensure it complies with their trading rules (e.g., no hedging, no grid strategies on forex).

Performance Analytics — AquaFunded tracks detailed trading stats: win rate, risk-reward ratio, average trade duration, most-traded pairs/assets, monthly drawdown curve, and Sharpe ratio. These analytics update daily and help you identify whether you're meeting the challenge benchmarks and which strategies are working. Export reports as PDFs for your records.

Risk Management Tools — The platform enforces hard stops at daily and maximum loss limits—you cannot trade further once these thresholds hit. Additionally, AquaFunded shows your real-time account equity, margin level, and margin available. Some traders use this real-time feedback to tighten their position sizes as they approach daily limits, preventing a disqualifying breach.

Account Scaling and Profit Splits — Once you pass a challenge and transition to a funded account, AquaFunded tracks your monthly profit. You receive your profit split (typically 70-90% trader, 10-30% firm, or higher if you purchase the 100% split add-on) and become eligible for account scaling. Each funded account can scale up to $2 million over time as you demonstrate consistent profitability.

AquaFunded Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Instant Funding ($19/month for $2.5K account) — This is AquaFunded's entry-level product. You get $2,500 in simulated capital, no profit target (you keep 100% of any profit immediately), and can begin trading on day one. However, Instant Funding is designed for scalping and small-account traders; the low capital means limited position sizing. Best for traders who want to try AquaFunded's platform with minimal risk or those building from very small accounts.

2-Step Challenge ($97/month for $10K account) — You must hit a 10% profit target on the first step, then a 5% profit target on the second step to earn funding. The challenge enforces a 5% daily loss limit and 10% maximum loss to prevent over-leverage. This tier attracts swing traders and part-time traders who trade 2-3 times per week and can afford to wait a few weeks for the evaluation. The pricing is reasonable compared to competitors like Funded Trader ($188 equivalent) and EverFunded ($155 equivalent).

1-Step Challenge ($199/month for $25K account) — Single evaluation phase with a 10% profit target. Only a 5% daily loss limit applies (strict), and you must pass in one phase. This suits experienced traders and full-time traders with proven strategies who are confident in one shot. The $25K capital allows meaningful position sizing in forex and indices, making real profit potential attractive.

2-Step Challenge ($549/month for $100K account) — AquaFunded's premium tier. First step: 8% profit target; second step: 5% profit target. Daily loss limit is typically 5% ($5,000), maximum loss is 10% ($10,000). The $100K account unlocks serious scaling potential and higher absolute profit per trade. This is aimed at professional traders and those who've already passed lower-tier challenges and want faster scaling. Pricing is competitive with The 5ers' $100K offerings.

Recommendation by trader type: Beginners should start with Instant Funding to learn the platform, then graduate to the 2-Step $10K if confident. Intermediate traders already passing challenges on other platforms should jump to 1-Step or 2-Step $25K. Advanced traders and full-time traders seeking maximum capital should tackle the 2-Step $100K or contact AquaFunded for custom higher tiers.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of AquaFunded

1. Study the challenge rules before paying — AquaFunded's trading rules differ slightly by challenge type. The 2-Step $10K and 1-Step $25K have stricter daily loss limits than some competitors. Download AquaFunded's full rule documentation and cross-reference it with your typical daily volatility and position size. If your usual strategy risking $500 per trade violates their 5% daily loss limit, you'll need to adjust before entering the challenge.

2. Use the 14-day holding period strategically — After passing your challenge and transitioning to funded, AquaFunded holds your first withdrawal for 14 days. During this window, continue trading profitably to build trust with the firm and qualify for faster scaling. If you withdraw after the 14 days, you'll likely reinvest in the next challenge tier anyway, so treat the holding period as an extension of your evaluation—not a pause.

3. Exploit the no-time-limit evaluation — Unlike FTMO (strict 30-day time limits) and Topstep (90-day limits), AquaFunded places no time restriction on reaching your profit target. If you trade intraday and hit your target in one week, great. If you're a swing trader taking 60 days, that's fine too. Use this flexibility to avoid rushing trades or over-leveraging just to hit a deadline.

4. Layer challenges for faster scaling — AquaFunded permits running multiple challenges simultaneously. Buy two $10K challenges, pass both, and scale to $20K funded capital faster than grinding through one tier at a time. This strategy requires more capital upfront but accelerates your path to the $2 million scaling cap.

5. Monitor the Trustpilot sentiment and recent updates — AquaFunded's Trustpilot profile shows mixed reviews and guideline breach flags. Before each challenge renewal, check Trustpilot and AquaFunded's social media for recent complaints (withdrawal delays, unexpected rule changes, firm instability). If negative reviews spike, it might signal operational issues.

6. Document your rules compliance from day one — Keep detailed trade logs (entry price, exit price, reasoning, position size as % of capital). If AquaFunded ever disputes a trade or withdrawal, having documented compliance proof protects you. Many traders ignore this until a dispute arises—don't be that trader.

7. Test your broker integration before your challenge starts — Ensure MetaTrader, cTrader, or your chosen platform connects correctly to AquaFunded's dashboard. Sometimes API connections drop or reporting delays occur. Run paper trades for 2-3 days before your challenge to confirm real-time P&L syncing works flawlessly.

Common AquaFunded Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue: "Hard breach—you've been disqualified" — You hit the maximum loss limit before passing your evaluation. This typically happens because traders underestimate volatility or over-size positions. If disqualified, you've forfeited your challenge fee. To avoid this, reduce position size by 50% on your next challenge attempt. Use AquaFunded's risk calculator in the dashboard to pre-plan position sizes that never exceed your daily and maximum loss limits even in worst-case scenarios.

Issue: Withdrawal delayed beyond 14 days — Most withdrawals clear within 24 hours after the 14-day holding period, but some traders report 5-7 day delays, especially on international bank transfers. If your withdrawal hasn't arrived after 10 business days, contact AquaFunded support with your withdrawal request ID. Request they expedite it and provide a timeline. In the meantime, continue trading on your funded account to build positive history with the firm.

Issue: Dashboard shows incorrect P&L or missing trades — Broker API integration sometimes lags or drops trades from the feed. First, restart your MetaTrader platform and refresh the AquaFunded dashboard (F5 or pull-to-refresh on mobile). Wait 5 minutes for re-sync. If trades are still missing, check your broker's statement directly to confirm they executed. Then submit a support ticket with a screenshot of both the missing trade and your broker's confirmation. AquaFunded usually corrects it within 1 business day.

Issue: Account frozen pending additional verification — Sometimes AquaFunded requests additional KYC documentation (deeper proof of address, tax ID, source of funds) mid-challenge or before first withdrawal. This is standard anti-money-laundering procedure, but it can feel stressful if you've just passed. Respond to their email immediately with requested documents. Most freezes clear within 48 hours of full documentation submission.

Is AquaFunded Worth It? Our Verdict

AquaFunded delivers on its core promise: affordable, multi-tier challenges with genuine scaling potential to $2 million funded capital and reasonable profit splits. The 3.6/5 rating and multiple challenge formats (especially Instant Funding at $19/month) make it accessible for traders of all experience levels, while the no-time-limit evaluation and mobile app appeal to part-time traders. However, the firm's 3.2/5 Trustpilot rating, historical guideline breaches, and 2023 founding date mean you're betting on a newer, less-proven firm compared to industry veterans like FTMO or Topstep. AquaFunded is worth trying if you're a swing trader or forex specialist seeking affordable entry-level challenges and don't mind accepting higher operational risk; experienced traders or those requiring rock-solid firm stability should compare it against FTMO, The 5ers, and AquaFunded vs Funded Trader before committing. Read the full AquaFunded review for detailed pros and cons.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
